key_drivers: Real yields falling sharply (DFII10 at 1.87%, down from 2.0%+ earlier in March, -13bps), the single strongest cross-asset bullish catalyst for gold, tech, and risk assets
Iran war/Strait of Hormuz blockade: largest oil supply disruption in IEA history driving geopolitical risk premium into commodities and safe havens; WTI at $95.55
RBA back-to-back hike to 4.10% today is bearish for 6A (risk-off + tightening); MOVE fell sharply -7.06% and VIX fell -4.85%, signaling easing volatility ahead of FOMC

stale_sources: FRED DFII10 (same-day, Mar 17 data — confirmed fresh)
fallbacks_used: Copper price from Yahoo Finance HG=F (Investing.com blocked by policy)
MOVE from TradingView TVC:MOVE (Yahoo Finance ^MOVE returned 500 error)
data_quality.notes: All primary data sources accessed fresh as of 2026-03-17 1930 EST. FRED DFII10 confirmed same-day (1.87%). VIX from Yahoo Finance (22.37, -4.85%). MOVE from TradingView (79.23, -7.06%). EIA data for week ending Mar 6 (latest available, released Mar 11). GDPNow as of Mar 13 (next update Mar 19). RBA hike confirmed via Reuters/ABC News today. Pre-FOMC and pre-BoJ meeting environment (both March 18-19) reduces confidence on rate-sensitive instruments by 1 point.
